title: Stipecoma peltata
taxonomy: Stipecoma peltata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is commonly known as the "butterfly flower" due to its unique appearance. Stipecoma peltata is a perennial herb that produces showy, funnel-shaped flowers in shades of yellow and orange. Its classification as a member of the Apocynaceae family places it among the dogbane and oleander family of plants.
uses: Stipecoma peltata is known to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems. Its leaves and roots are rich in bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in developing new medicines, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Traditional healers in the region where Stipecoma peltata grows use its extracts to treat a range of ailments.
